track_description: Background boiler and steam-system housekeeping improvements that reduce incumbent fossil heat demand without changing the carrier.
evidence_summary: Australian industrial-efficiency planning evidence supports a modest autonomous background improvement in low-temperature fossil heat systems.
derivation_method: Thin-slice calibration: keep the fossil base row flat at the 2025 gas and combustion-emissions intensity, then carry the previously implicit later-year improvement through the explicit autonomous track.
double_counting_guardrail: Applies only to the fossil incumbent and de-embeds the matching drift from family_states.csv so the autonomous effect is explicit rather than double counted.
review_notes: Calibration-first thin slice to prove the canonical path; revisit effect size after low-temperature heat is split into narrower process families.
